Question: State in your own words what it means for a function $$f$$ to be continuous at $$x = c .$$
Answer: Informally, if a function is continuous at $$x = c ,$$ then there is no break in the graph of the function at $$f ( c ) ,$$ and $$f ( c )$$ is defined.

Question: State in your own words what it means for a function to be continuous on the interval $$( a , b ) .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \ln{} | x + 3 | , a = - 3$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \ln{} | 5 x - 2 | , a = \frac{2}{5}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{x^{2} - 16}{x + 4} , a = - 4$$
Answer: removable discontinuity at $$a = - 4$$ ; $$f ( - 4 )$$ is not defined

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{x^{2} - 16 x}{x} , a = 0$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = \left{\right. x , x \neq 3 \\ 2 x , x = 3 a = 3$$
Answer: Discontinuous at $$a = 3$$ ; $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 3 \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = 3 ,$$ but $$f ( 3 ) = 6 ,$$ which is not equal to the limit.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = \left{\right. 5 , x \neq 0 \\ 3 , x = 0 a = 0$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}\frac{1}{2 - x} , & x \ne 2 \\ 3 , & x = 2\end{matrix}\right . a = 2$$
Answer: $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 2 \right ) }{lim} f ( x )$$ does not exist.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}\frac{1}{x + 6} , & x = - 6 \\ x^{2} , & x \ne - 6\end{matrix}\right . a = - 6$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}3 + x , & x < 1 \\ x , & x = 1 \\ x^{2} , & x > 1\end{matrix}\right . \text{quadquad} a = 1$$
Answer: $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 1^{-} \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = 4 ; \underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 1^{+} \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = 1$$ . Therefore, $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 1 \right ) }{lim} f ( x )$$ does not exist.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}3 - x , & x < 1 \\ x , & x = 1 \\ 2 x^{2} , & x > 1\end{matrix}\right . \text{quadquad} a = 1$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}3 + 2 x , & x < 1 \\ x , & x = 1 \\ - x^{2} , & x > 1\end{matrix}\right . \text{quadquad} a = 1$$
Answer: $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 1^{-} \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = 5 \ne \underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 1^{+} \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = - 1$$ . Thus $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 1 \right ) }{lim} f ( x )$$ does not exist.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x^{2} , & x < - 2 \\ 2 x + 1 , & x = - 2 \\ x^{3} , & x > - 2\end{matrix}\right . \text{quadquad} a = - 2$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}\frac{x^{2} - 9}{x + 3} , & x < - 3 \\ x - 9 , & x = - 3 \\ \frac{1}{x} , & x > - 3\end{matrix}\right . \text{quadquad} a = - 3$$
Answer: $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} - 3^{-} \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = - 6$$ , $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} - 3^{+} \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = - \frac{1}{3}$$ Therefore, $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} - 3 \right ) }{lim} f ( x )$$ does not exist.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine why the function $$f$$ is discontinuous at a given point $$a$$ on the graph. State which condition fails.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}\frac{x^{2} - 9}{x + 3} , & x < - 3 \\ x - 9 , & x = - 3 \\ - 6 , & x > - 3\end{matrix}\right . \text{quadquad} a = 3$$

Question: Find the limit $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 1 \right ) }{lim} f ( x )$$ and determine if the following function is continuous at $$x = 1 :$$ $$f x = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x^{2} + 4 & x \ne 1 \\ 2 & x = 1\end{matrix}\right .$$
Answer: The function is discontinuous at $$x = 1$$ because the limit as $$x$$ approaches 1 is 5 and $$f ( 1 ) = 2.$$
